prerequisites: Your numeric Acuity User ID, found in Acuity under Integrations.
Your Acuity API Key, found in Acuity under Integrations.
A start date to use as the beginning of the data replication window.

authMethods.name: HTTP Basic Auth (User ID and API Key)
authMethods.summary: Acuity authenticates over SSL using HTTP Basic Auth: your numeric User ID is the username and your API Key is the password. Set the connector's username field to your User ID and the password (secret) field to your API Key. A failed authentication returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized.

setupSteps.title: Locate your API credentials in Acuity
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Acuity Scheduling and find your API credentials under Integrations. Acuity displays your numeric User ID and your API Key there. Copy both and store them securely.

setupSteps.title: Enter the username (User ID)
setupSteps.body: Set the connector's username field to your numeric Acuity User ID. This value is used as the Basic Auth username.

setupSteps.title: Enter the password (API Key)
setupSteps.body: Set the connector's password field (a secret) to your Acuity API Key. This value is used as the Basic Auth password.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the date from which the connector should begin replicating data. Records dated before this point are not synced.

setupSteps.title: Verify the connection
setupSteps.body: Save and test the connection. The connector authenticates against the Acuity API (for example GET https://acuityscheduling.com/api/v1/appointments). If credentials are incorrect, Acuity returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized; re-check the User ID and API Key from the Integrations page.
